item.title: UN Security Council to convene emergency meeting on Russian missile terror
item.description: Ukraine requested an emergency UN Security Council meeting for June 8 in response to Russia recent large-scale ballistic missile attacks on Ukrainian cities. Foreign Minister Sybiha said Moscow continues choosing escalation over peace.

item.title: Iran FM rejects Lebanese interference accusations, says focus on real foe
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi dismissed Lebanese President Joseph Aoun accusation of interference, telling him to focus on the real foe as Lebanon leaders call for Iran to stop interfering in their affairs.

item.title: China mobilizes SOEs and tech firms to hire record 12.7 million graduates
item.description: Eight government departments launched a nationwide campaign requiring state-owned enterprises and major private tech firms to expand hiring for the class of 2026, marking one of China broadest labor market interventions in recent years.

item.title: KMT leader says Taiwan can preserve peace by avoiding de jure independence
item.description: Kuomintang Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun told a Harvard seminar that cross-strait peace can be maintained as long as Taipei does not cross the red line of de jure independence, advocating for both deterrence and dialogue frameworks.

item.title: Pope Leo XIV visits polarized Spain with focus on migrants and social justice
item.description: Pope Leo XIV weeklong visit to Spain brings him to a once-staunchly Catholic country now grappling with secularization and political polarization, with immigration and social justice as central themes.

item.title: Armenia heads to polls in election that could reshape ties with Moscow and the West
item.description: Armenia holds parliamentary elections Sunday as Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan seeks closer Western ties while opposition parties favor Russia. The vote could determine Armenia's geopolitical alignment in the South Caucasus.

item.title: Macron, Starmer and Merz to meet Zelenskiy in London for Ukraine talks
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz will hold talks with Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskiy on Sunday, according to the French presidency.

item.title: Putin says he sees no reason to meet Zelenskiy after Ukrainian peace overture
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said he currently saw no reason to meet Volodymyr Zelenskiy after the Ukrainian president published an open letter proposing face-to-face talks to end the war now in its fifth year.

item.title: Iran reaffirms support for Hezbollah as broader peace deal faces complications
item.description: Iran has reaffirmed support for its Lebanese ally Hezbollah and demanded Israel withdraw from southern Lebanon, underscoring the complications facing an interim deal to end the broader conflict between the U.S. and Iran.

item.title: Five civilians killed in Russian attacks on Ukraine's Kherson region
item.description: Russian attacks killed five people in Ukraine's southern Kherson region in three separate incidents on Friday, according to the regional governor, as fighting continues along the front lines.

item.title: Pope Leo visits Spain with focus on migrants and political polarization
item.description: Pope Leo begins a week-long visit to Spain, his first to an EU country outside Italy, where he will inaugurate a new tower at Barcelona's Sagrada Familia and meet with migrants who crossed the Atlantic to reach Europe.

item.title: Putin holds friendly meeting with former German Chancellor Schroeder
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin held a one-on-one meeting with former German Chancellor Gerhard Schroeder, described as "good and friendly" by Kremlin aide Yuri Ushakov, amid ongoing tensions with the West.

item.title: UK house prices fall unexpectedly as Iran war impact hits market
item.description: British house prices unexpectedly fell in May according to Halifax data, as higher borrowing costs and uncertainty from the Iran conflict weighed on demand.

item.title: Canada reports strong job growth with unemployment rate falling to 6.6%
item.description: Canada's economy added 87,800 jobs in May with unemployment falling to 6.6%, defying expectations and showing economic resilience despite signs of slower growth.

item.title: Turkish fishing boat attacked in Black Sea, one sailor killed
item.description: A Turkish-flagged fishing boat was attacked and sank off the northern Black Sea coast, leaving one sailor dead and four wounded. The Turkish Coast Guard is investigating the incident.

item.title: OPEC Output Hits Decades-Low as US Blockade Squeezes Iran
item.description: OPEC crude production fell to its lowest level in decades as the US maritime blockade of Iran disrupts exports, tightening global supply.

item.title: Iran Dodges US Blockade Through Iraq as New Smuggling Route Emerges
item.description: More than 50 days after the US-led maritime blockade, Iraq's Umm Qasr port has become a key smuggling route for Iran to circumvent sanctions.

item.title: Iran Launches Missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain, US Military Says
item.description: Iran launched 7 missiles targeting US military bases in Kuwait and Bahrain, marking a significant escalation as Gulf states intercept incoming attacks.

item.title: US and Iran Exchange Strikes in Gulf as Ceasefire Efforts Stall
item.description: US forces struck Iranian coastal radar sites after shooting down drones near the Strait of Hormuz, while Iran targeted US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain in a new escalation complicating peace efforts.

item.title: UAE Stock Markets Rally Despite Stalled US-Iran Peace Talks
item.description: Dubai markets outperformed regional peers on Friday, with investors appearing to brush aside fading hopes for a diplomatic breakthrough in the US-Iran conflict.

item.title: China Invests $162 Million in AI to Promote Xi Jinping Ideology
item.description: Xinhuanet plans to invest over 1.1 billion yuan on an authoritative AI agent to help promote President Xi Jinping political thinking, according to Shanghai Stock Exchange filings.

item.title: Iran World Cup Players Granted US Visas Despite Ongoing Conflict
item.description: Iran's World Cup soccer players have been granted visas to enter the United States, a White House official confirmed, just 10 days before their first match in Los Angeles amid the ongoing conflict between the two countries.

item.title: US Strikes Iranian Radar Sites After Drone Launches Near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US forces struck Iranian coastal radar sites on Qeshm Island after shooting down drones launched by Iran toward the Strait of Hormuz, CENTCOM said. The escalation complicates ongoing diplomatic efforts to end the conflict.

item.title: Ukraine and Russia Exchange 185 Prisoners Each in Latest POW Swap
item.description: Ukraine and Russia each exchanged 185 service personnel in the latest prisoner of war swap on Friday, officials in both countries said, continuing the pattern of periodic exchanges despite the ongoing conflict.

item.title: China's Xi Jinping to Visit North Korea in First Trip in Nearly 7 Years
item.description: China announced President Xi Jinping will visit North Korea on a two-day trip from June 8, his first in nearly seven years, as Beijing seeks to reassert ties with Pyongyang, its only formal treaty ally.
